description: Trigger discrimination: the operator-executable DATA-move runbook (this skill) vs the change sequence (schema-evolution-planner), DDL safety review (secure-migration-reviewer), general release rollback (rollback-runbook-author), and the reusable gated-op prompt template (gated-deployment-prompt-template).

cases.prompt: Turn the approved re-shard plan into the step-by-step operator procedure: batch sizes with throttling, the verification queries after each batch, abort thresholds, and where the human sign-offs go.
cases.reason: An APPROVED plan needing operational HOW — batching, per-batch verification, aborts, approval markers — is exactly the runbook layer. The sequence is decided, no DDL review is requested, and this is one specific move, not a reusable template.

cases.prompt: We want to move the address fields out of the users table into their own table without breaking deploys — figure out the staged sequence and compatibility windows.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — plan vs runbook, seen from the runbook side: no approved plan exists yet, so there is nothing to operationalize. Expand/migrate/contract staging and compatibility guarantees come first; this runbook skill is its downstream consumer.
cases.reason: The ask is the change sequence itself. The runbook author's Stop Conditions require an approved plan as a prerequisite — invoking it here would fail its own gate.

cases.prompt: Before this backfill migration ships, check it: lock and downtime risk, whether the new table has RLS, and if the backfill bypasses tenant scope.
cases.reason: Lock risk, RLS enablement gaps, and tenant-scope bypass in a written migration are the safety REVIEW — whose verdict the runbook later cites as a prerequisite. Review and runbook are different layers on purpose.

cases.prompt: Author the backout runbook for the release: blue/green flip back, flag kill, comms steps, and the decision time-box for rollback versus fix-forward.
cases.note: SEAM CASE — runbook vs runbook: the subject is a RELEASE (artifact/flag/traffic primitives), not a data move. The data-move runbook COMPOSES rollback-runbook-author's conventions for its rollback sections; a pure release backout belongs to that skill wholesale.
cases.reason: Blue/green flips, flag kills, and release backout criteria are release-rollback craft with no batching, parity, or data-cutover content.

cases.prompt: We run roughly the same risky data-sync operation monthly. Build the reusable operator prompt template with placeholders, hard rules, stop conditions, and the required per-run report.
cases.reason: A RECURRING operation needing a placeholder-driven prompt template with per-run reporting is the template skill's form. A one-off move gets this skill's full runbook; the two compose when a rehearsed runbook graduates into a monthly template.
